title: Tender Crunchy Thai Coconut Khanom Thai
description: A sour, crunchy, and comforting Thai dish featuring heavy cream and coconut, perfect for a snack or dessert. This Buddhist-temple-friendly recipe is made with love and care.
instructions: Combine coconut milk, heavy cream, and palm sugar in a pressure cooker and cook on high for 10 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together rice flour, water, and vegetable oil to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into small balls and flatten each ball into a disk shape.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat and fry the disks until they are crispy and golden brown.
Drain the fried disks on paper towels and sprinkle with salt and tapioca starch.
Serve the crunchy khanom thai with the creamy coconut sauce.
Garnish with toasted coconut flakes and fresh herbs if desired.
